clauses.statement: Delivery policy preserves unspecified limits and unmeasured values as unknown, evaluates time, token, cost, subagent, role-dispatch, repair-batch, and verification budgets, and emits typed budget_exceeded or attribution_unknown stops.

test_refs.case: policy keeps unspecified and unmeasured budgets unknown instead of zero
budget exceed and required attribution unknown are typed stops

clauses.statement: Before provider spawn, review authorization records intended model, risk closure, expected judgment delta, reusable evidence and a Story-wide budget snapshot under one lock; final dispatch requires a frozen source, Spec, test and review surface. Active reservations and running, uncollected or completed-pass lifecycles make the Story-stage-role-HEAD-surface binding idempotent, and review start consumes one matching authorization exactly once.

test_refs.case: final review waits for an exact frozen surface while preflight remains available
same binding dispatch is idempotent for running, uncollected, and completed pass lifecycles
review authorize prevents spawn before evidence, freeze, Story-wide budget, and idempotency checks

clauses.statement: Running review work superseded by a HEAD mutation becomes obsolete only after confirmed cancellation or closure; unconfirmed cancellation emits an orphaned_agent stop.

test_refs.case: HEAD mutation terminalizes obsolete work and fails closed when cancellation is unconfirmed
review lifecycle fails closed on HEAD mutation and persists obsolete after explicit close

clauses.statement: Compatible repairable findings with one role and normalized code/test surface share one implementation dispatch, targeted verification and independent re-review; conflicts and human or split decisions remain separate.

test_refs.case: compatible repairable findings batch by role and surface while human/conflicting findings remain separate
TDEG-S-5 compatible repairable findings share one dispatch, verification, and re-review batch

clauses.statement: Story metrics separate Trusted PR-ready elapsed, observed work, tool wait, union review wall-clock, summed agent consumption, subagent and role dispatch counts, repair and verification counters, evidence reuse or invalidation, fresh and total tokens and cost; missing values remain unknown.

test_refs.case: metrics separate review union wall clock from parallel agent consumption and preserve unknown
SRP-S-6 summary reports per-Story time cost suite reuse and interruptions without converting unknown to zero
portfolio aggregates overlapping review intervals before persisting closed attribution

clauses.statement: PR correctness readiness remains independent from visible timeout, obsolete, orphan, duplicate-dispatch and budget efficiency debt; required tests, independent review, critical Gates, current-HEAD binding and fail-closed semantics are never relaxed for efficiency.

test_refs.case: efficiency debt stays separate from correctness readiness
pr gate summary exposes review lifecycle debt without changing correctness readiness
pr summary exposes delivery budget, attribution, dispatch, repair, and debt without changing correctness readiness

clauses.statement: Given delivery measurements are missing, when a Story budget is evaluated, then unknown values remain unknown and are never converted to zero.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given a review has no concrete risk closure or judgment delta, when dispatch is evaluated, then the guardrail returns a typed stop instead of spawning by role presence alone.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given source, Spec, tests, or review surface are not frozen, when final review is requested, then dispatch is blocked while preflight remains available.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given an identical Story, stage, role, HEAD, and surface binding already exists, when dispatch is requested again, then running, uncollected, or completed-pass work is reused rather than duplicated.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given compatible repairable findings share a role and code-test surface, when repair is planned, then they receive one mutation batch, targeted verification, and independent re-review while conflicts remain separate.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given a running reviewer belongs to an older HEAD, when close is attempted without explicit cancellation confirmation and evidence, then it remains orphaned and fail closed; confirmed cancellation terminalizes it as obsolete.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given budget exhaustion, no progress, unknown attribution, or an orphaned agent, when the next action is evaluated, then a typed stop prevents additional spawn and cannot become success, waiver, or Gate pass.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given correctness Gates pass but lifecycle efficiency debt remains, when PR readiness is prepared, then correctness and timeout, obsolete, orphan, duplicate, and budget debt are displayed separately.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given overlapping parallel reviews and partial usage data, when Story metrics are aggregated, then union wait, summed agent consumption, counters, tokens, cost, and unknown fields remain distinct.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given no comparable pre-change workflow-heavy measurement exists, when performance evidence is recorded, then before remains unknown, the run establishes a baseline, and no numeric improvement claim or changed-line allocation is made.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given repeated same-role review, repair batches, HEAD mutation, timeout, orphan, budget stop, parallel review, and unknown metrics, when the acceptance replay runs, then each path has executable evidence.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

clauses.statement: Given the efficiency guardrail is enabled, when existing validation, review lifecycle, PR readiness, and Design SSOT contract suites run, then required tests, independent final review, critical Gates, current-HEAD binding, and fail-closed semantics remain intact.

test_refs.case: trusted delivery efficiency guardrail replays TDEG-S-1 through TDEG-S-12

diagrams.title: Review cancellation trust boundary
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Parent[Parent coordinator] --> Close[review close]
  Provider[Provider cancellation result] --> Confirm[Explicit confirmation plus evidence]
  Head[Current HEAD] --> Close
  Close -->|same HEAD| Closed[Closed lifecycle]
  Close -->|changed HEAD unconfirmed| Orphan[Orphaned agent fail closed]
  Confirm --> Obsolete[Obsolete terminal state]
  Close --> Obsolete
